expectations.text: Identifies that pure-FP Haskell has no runtime object identity — equal values are indistinguishable — and flags this as a problem the integrator must solve, not assume away
expectations.evidence: No mention of identity, no discussion of value semantics. The agent treats the problem as 'serialize Haskell to JSON, then call sPyTial in Python' and never engages with what identity would mean for atoms in a relational view.

expectations.text: Names at least one concrete strategy for supplying identity in Haskell: hash-consing, structural hash with cycle detection, path-from-root, explicit ID field, or System.Mem.StableName / observable sharing
expectations.evidence: No identity strategy proposed at all. Skipped entirely.

expectations.text: Picks a Haskell-idiomatic spec-attachment seam: type-class instances (e.g. class Spytial a), Generic deriving, or a separate Spec ADT — and contrasts with non-natural choices
expectations.evidence: No native Haskell spec-attachment seam proposed. Suggests using sPyTial's Python-side annotations (AnnotatedType, InferredEdge, Orientation) on the Python mirror dataclasses, after JSON-bridging from Haskell.

expectations.text: Names AST as the classical example for Subproblem 4 (Gaps) — the user explicitly said AST trees, so the design must engage with it concretely
expectations.evidence: AST is named, code example uses Node{ctor, children}, and Option 2 mirrors a Recipe ADT in Python. Engages with the AST shape, even if via a bridge architecture.

expectations.text: Addresses ordering of child lists (insertion order, position-as-relation, or orderBy directive) — important for ASTs whose children have semantic positions
expectations.evidence: Ordering is not discussed. The Python-side example uses lists for children but no mention of how Spytial preserves or expresses that order.

expectations.text: States a path to emit JSON conforming to JSONDataInstance, with Aeson or hand-rolled serialization
expectations.evidence: Emits JSON with aeson, but it is *aeson's default ToJSON* shape — a tagged-sum encoding of the user's AST — not the JSONDataInstance shape (atoms / relations / types). The agent never discovered that spytial-core has a JSON contract; it bridges through Python sPyTial instead.

expectations.text: Acknowledges the user is a beginner — is welcoming, doesn't dump 4 phases of jargon without scaffolding, and offers a simple first step
expectations.evidence: Opens with 'Short answer up front'. Three options ranked from least to most effort. Practical tips section. Genuinely friendly.

expectations.text: Provides a path to a minimum-viable build (JSON emitter + HTML harness rendering one AST example), not just abstract design
expectations.evidence: Option 1 has a complete code path: aeson encode -> json.load -> spytial.diagram. Three working code blocks. Suggests Jupyter for tight inner loop.
